sections.paragraph:
ἀπορρέω pass.: fut. ἀπορυήσομαι and 2 aor. 3 sg. ἀπερρύη (s. ῥέω; Trag., Hdt. et al.; Eudoxos-Papyrus [=PParis 1] 14, 328 [II b.c.]; LXX, Just., Ath.) lit. ‘flow down’; fig., of leaves (Demosth. 22, 70; cp. Sb 7350 of hair falling out) fall down B 11:6, 8 (Ps 1:3).

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PParis PParis = Notices et textes des papyrus (Paris)—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 119
